sanskrit:
अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः।
तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः॥
transliteration:
ananya-cetāḥ satataṁ yo māṁ smarati nityaśaḥ
tasyāhaṁ sulabhaḥ pārtha nitya-yuktasya yoginaḥ
literal_meaning: Without-other-minded / constantly / who / Me / remembers / ever / to-him / I / easily-available / O-Pārtha / ever-yoked / of-the-yogi
contextual_meaning: When attention is single-pointedly anchored to one internal object of devotion, the nervous system stops burning energy on conflicting goals; what was ‘distant’ becomes experientially immediate.
psychological_state: Flow-state sustained by exclusive object constancy; ego boundaries soften without threat because the object is internalized as benevolent.
trigger_scenario: A recovering workaholic who, instead of checking emails during breakfast, spends five mindful minutes looking at a photo of her child—suddenly the whole day feels ‘doable’.
anchor_text: Keep your mind quietly fixed on Me and I am already with you.
